title: Dual Aging Conferences Open Today: On Aging 2026 in Atlanta and Global Congress in Dubai
description: Two major aging conferences launch simultaneously on April 20. The American Society on Aging's On Aging 2026 convenes thousands of professionals at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta through April 23 with the theme 'The Power of Belonging.' Meanwhile, the 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology opens in Dubai, focusing on 'The Future of Aging: Science, Sustainability, and Society.' Together they represent an unprecedented week for multidisciplinary aging discourse.

title: FDA-Approved Leukemia Drug HHT Extends Mouse Lifespan by Clearing Senescent Fat Cells
description: Homoharringtonine (HHT), already FDA-approved for leukemia, has been identified as a potent senotherapeutic that clears senescent fat cells, restores metabolic function, and extends lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice. Published in Nature Communications, this finding from a screen of 2,150 approved drugs could accelerate the path to human senolytic trials given HHT's existing safety profile.

title: Engineered Blood Stem Cells Create Lifelong Protein Factories in Breakthrough Published in Science
description: Rockefeller University researchers genetically engineered hematopoietic stem cells to produce B cells that churn out rare broad-action antibodies against HIV, malaria, and flu indefinitely. Published in Science, this platform could potentially be adapted for any therapeutic protein, opening doors for lifelong treatments for metabolic diseases and age-related protein deficiencies with a single injection.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Forge $2.75 Billion AI-Driven Drug Discovery Collaboration
description: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly announced a global R&D collaboration worth up to $2.75 billion, using Insilico's generative AI engine to accelerate novel therapeutic discovery across multiple areas. This deal, building on two previous agreements, represents one of the largest AI-pharma partnerships and signals growing Big Pharma confidence in AI-driven longevity drug pipelines.

title: Buck Institute's THRIVE Initiative Secures $34.5M from ARPA-H to Build First FDA-Grade Aging Score
description: A Stanford-led coalition including the Buck Institute and Methuselah Foundation received up to $34.5 million from ARPA-H's PROSPR program to develop the first FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity Score for measuring and predicting healthy aging. The initiative will run 1,000+ person trials through YMCA partnerships, aiming to create diagnostic infrastructure that proves aging is quantifiable and treatable.

title: Homoharringtonine (HHT) Identified as Senotherapeutic Drug That Extends Lifespan in Mice
description: A screen of 2,150 clinically approved drugs identified HHT as a senotherapeutic compound that clears senescent adipocytes, restores white adipose tissue function, lowers inflammation, and extends lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice.

title: Engineered Stem Cells Become Lifelong Protein Factories Against HIV, Malaria, and Flu
description: Rockefeller University researchers engineered hematopoietic stem cells to produce B cells that churn out rare broad-action antibodies. As few as 29 edited cells produced measurable antibody levels, and the platform could be adapted for any therapeutic protein.

title: On Aging 2026 Opens April 20-23 in Atlanta: The Nation's Largest Multidisciplinary Aging Conference
description: On Aging 2026, themed 'The Power of Belonging,' brings together thousands of professionals across public health, research, policy, and aging services at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta.

title: 11th Global Congress on Aging and Gerontology Opens in Dubai April 20-21
description: The hybrid event themed 'The Future of Aging: Science, Sustainability, and Society' covers cellular and molecular repair, social implications of aging, and emerging longevity technologies.

title: ITP Reports 11 Compounds Including Astaxanthin and Alpha-Ketoglutarate Fail to Extend Mouse Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program tested 11 compounds in UM-HET3 mice across three sites. None extended lifespan, and some were associated with reduced lifespan in females.

title: FDA-Approved Drugs Terbinafine and Miglustat Extend C. elegans Lifespan via UPRmt Activation
description: Two FDA-approved drugs, terbinafine (antifungal) and miglustat (Gaucher disease), extend worm lifespan by activating the mitochondrial unfolded protein response, with existing safety profiles that could accelerate human trials.

title: Tau Seeds Propagate Through Brain via Neural Connectivity, Not Physical Proximity
description: New research shows tau protein seeds spread through the brain following neural connectivity patterns rather than simple physical proximity, offering insights into Alzheimer's progression and potential intervention targets.

title: Longevity Segment Investability Tracker: Cellular Reprogramming Leads With $1.28B Raised
description: Two-year tracker shows cellular reprogramming at $1.28B (6 deals), age-related disease drugs at $835M (11 deals), and preventive health at $542M. Insilico Medicine alone accumulated $3.6B in pharma deal value.

title: Central European BioForum 2026: 'Longevity and Ageing -- The Next Frontier for European VC'
description: A dedicated panel at CEBioForum2026 will explore longevity as an investable European VC thesis, covering how aging biology translates into investment strategies and realistic partnering and exit pathways.

title: 4 Healthy Aging Habits a Longevity Doctor Follows Most Days
description: A longevity-focused physician shares her daily anti-aging habits including strength training, sleep optimization with testing for sleep apnea, and intermittent fasting as core pillars.

title: CNN's 'Kara Swisher Wants to Live Forever' Explores Longevity Science Weekly
description: The six-episode CNN Original Series follows journalist Kara Swisher on a personal journey into longevity science, the booming business of life extension, and humanity's quest to cheat death.

title: AGE Meeting 2026 in Utah to Cover Biomarkers, AI in Aging, and New Longevity Tech
description: The American Aging Association's annual meeting will feature sessions on aging biomarkers, AI applications in aging research, and emerging longevity technologies.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Announce $2.75B AI-Driven R&D Collaboration
description: Building on two previous deals, Lilly licensed assets from Insilico's AI-driven pipeline in a collaboration potentially worth up to $2.75 billion across multiple therapeutic areas.

title: Buck Institute THRIVE Initiative: $34.5M to Build First FDA-Grade Intrinsic Capacity Score
description: Stanford-led coalition with Buck Institute, Methuselah Foundation, and YMCA secured $34.5M from ARPA-H to develop an FDA-grade aging measurement tool through 1,000+ person decentralized trials.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Trial Funded by NIA
description: The NIA-funded randomized, placebo-controlled trial of approximately 84 older adults will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and both short- and longer-term effects on healthy aging.

title: PEARL Trial Update: Rapamycin Shifts From Theoretical to Experimental in Human Aging Research
description: 2026 analysis notes rapamycin research shifting from theoretical to experimental, with the PEARL trial showing safety at low intermittent doses and modest improvements in immune function and lean tissue mass.

title: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by About Four Months Over Two Years
description: The COSMOS randomized clinical trial found daily multivitamin use slowed five measures of epigenetic aging by approximately four months over two years, with greater benefits for those already biologically older.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Covering 800,000+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses screening of 2,352 compounds for senolytic activity and neural networks trained to predict activity across 800,000+ molecules targeting Bcl-2 proteins.

title: Longevity Center Europe Study: Participants Aging 22% Slower Than Average Population
description: Over 80 individuals tested using DunedinPACE showed an average pace of aging of 0.78, meaning 22% slower than the general population. At least 10 clients are aging slower than Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, Up 56% Year-Over-Year
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events totaling $3.74B, a 56% increase over Q1 2025. Full-year projections converge on $8-9B, implying 55-60% growth as later-stage VC rounds take larger shares.

title: Life Biosciences' ER-100 Trial: Is the FDA Warming to Rejuvenation Therapies?
description: The FDA cleared Life Biosciences' IND filing for ER-100, an epigenetic treatment for partial cellular reprogramming in the human eye, signaling the FDA may be establishing a route for longevity interventions.

title: Blueprint $60M, Function Health $298M, Aeovian $55M: Recent Longevity Market Funding
description: Recent longevity funding includes Blueprint's $60M backed by Kim Kardashian and Logan Paul, Function Health's $298M Series B, and Aeovian's $55M Series B for mTORC1 inhibitors.

title: David Sinclair Teases Exciting April 2026 Announcement With Lifespan.io
description: Longevity researcher David Sinclair posted 'Exciting! April 2026' referencing Lifespan.io, hinting at an upcoming announcement or collaboration in the longevity space.

title: Buck Institute Launches Healthspan Horizons: AI-Powered Federated Data Framework for Aging
description: The Buck Institute unveiled Healthspan Horizons, using responsible AI and federated longitudinal data to identify early signs of aging decline. The platform enables analysis across partner environments without centralizing data ownership.

title: Clearing Senescent Cells Reduces T Cell Senescence and Inhibits Inflammaging
description: New research demonstrates that clearing senescent cells can reduce T cell senescence and inhibit inflammaging, supporting senolytic interventions for immune system rejuvenation.
